heading: Omitted
label: 220 to 224 Omitted
notes: CodificationSections 220 to 224, relating to Territory of Alaska, were omitted in view of admission of Alaska into the Union.Section 220, , , vested control over the fur seal, salmon and other fisheries of Alaska in Department of the Interior.act Feb. 14, 1903, ch. 552, § 732 Stat. 828Section 221, acts , ; , , empowered Secretary to set aside fishing areas, prescribed closed seasons, and to place limitations on catch.June 6, 1924, ch. 272, § 143 Stat. 464June 18, 1926, ch. 62144 Stat. 752Section 222, acts , ; , , declared that fishing in certain areas was unlawful, prohibited granting of exclusive rights, and provided that citizens shall not be denied fishing rights.June 6, 1924, ch. 272, § 143 Stat. 464June 18, 1926, ch. 62144 Stat. 752Section 222a, acts , ; , ; , , prohibited commercial salmon fishing by nonresidents.June 6, 1924, ch. 272, § 143 Stat. 464Aug. 14, 1937, ch. 62250 Stat. 639Apr. 7, 1938, ch. 11052 Stat. 208Section 223, acts , ; , , related to prohibited areas in creeks, streams, rivers, etc.June 6, 1924, ch. 272, § 143 Stat. 464June 18, 1926, ch. 62144 Stat. 752Section 223a, acts , ; , , authorized Secretary to permit taking of fish or shellfish for bait purposes.June 6, 1924, ch. 272, § 143 Stat. 464June 18, 1926, ch. 62144 Stat. 752Section 223b, acts , ; , , authorized Secretary to lease bottoms for oyster cultivation.June 6, 1924, ch. 272, § 143 Stat. 464Aug. 2, 1937, ch. 55650 Stat. 557Section 224, acts , ; , , prohibited importation of salmon during closed seasons.June 6, 1924, ch. 272, § 143 Stat. 464June 18, 1926, ch. 62144 Stat. 752
